title: Frankenstein Identity: Valid-But-Never-Married Data Elements Create New Person
narrative: Fraudster assembles identity from legitimate but unrelated data fragmentsâ€”real SSN from data breach, real address from skip-tracing site, real phone from previous account, newly created email. Each element validates individually (SSN passes Equifax soft pull, address confirms via USPS, phone has 3-year history) but combination never existed together. Account opens with $47 initial deposit, lies dormant 45-60 days establishing aged history. First transaction is $892 purchase at electronics MCC 5732, followed by $1,340 and $2,105 within 72 hours. Key tell: P_emaildomain creation date (domain_registration_days < 90) contradicts phone_age_days > 1100. Address shows 12+ previous occupants in credit bureau data (addr1_occupant_count high) but zero shared transaction history with the SSN. Card velocity explodes from 0 to 11 transactions in 96 hours after dormancy.
indicators: P_emaildomain registration < 90 days while phone_age_days > 1000 (temporal mismatch)
addr1_occupant_count > 10 with zero shared SSN transaction history
Account dormant 45-60 days then card1_txn_count_7d jumps from 0 to 8+
First three transactions: $892, $1340, $2105 all at electronics/jewelry MCCs within 72 hours
SSN soft-pull passes but SSN + addr1 combination returns zero credit tradelines
dist1 (billing-to-IP distance) > 500 km on activation but < 5 km on fraud transactions
card1_amt_zscore leaps from 0.1 to 4.8+ within first week of activity
summary: All identity components validate individually (SSN passes checks, address confirms via USPS, phone has multi-year history), but the specific combination of SSN, address, phone, and email has never appeared together in any credit tradeline or transaction history. The newly-registered email domain (under 90 days old) contradicts the aged phone (3+ years), and the address shows 10+ previous occupants with zero overlap to this SSN.
reasoning: The fraudster is stitching together real but unrelated data pieces stolen from breaches and public records to bypass individual validation checks while creating a person who never existed. After establishing legitimacy through a dormancy period, they rapidly drain value through high-dollar electronics purchases before the synthetic identity's internal contradictions trigger fraud detection.
indicator_explanations: New email paired with old phone suggests identity components were stitched together
Address houses many residents but none share transaction history with this identity
Account sits idle then suddenly explodes with dense transaction activity
Opening purchases are high-value items easily resold for cash within days
Social Security number validates alone but has no credit history at this address
Activation occurs from distant location but fraud happens near the billing address
Spending pattern rockets from normal to extreme in the account's first week
